Solana Foundation is proud to join the x402 Foundation as a founding member: 

“Open payment standards like @x402 are the starting point for building an agentic economy. As it scales, the importance of permissionless, scalable payment rails becomes evident, and we are focused on solving these problems on Solana.”

– @_rishinsharma, Head of AI Growth, @SolanaFndn
https://t.co/5pzFNGISQ4

AI agents need a native way to pay. Today, we announced the operational launch of the x402 Foundation to help enable community-based innovation in open payments with initial support from 40 member organizations and institutions. \n\nLearn more and join the effort: https://t.co/vmD1uWny1j

54 image models. 27 video models. Powered by x402.

Stablecoins are becoming more than another payment option; they're the foundation for modern payments and a strategic priority for businesses.\n\nSpeaking with the @ConvergeDefiant podcast, @brian__foster explains why the conversation has shifted from if businesses will adopt stablecoins to when, and how @Coinbase helps enterprises build products with stablecoins seamlessly working in the background:

When a broken DNSSEC rollover took down the .AL top-level domain, we deployed a Negative Trust Anchor on 1.1.1.1 to keep sites reachable. Now, clients do not have to guess if validation was skipped: 1.1.1.1 returns EDE 33, a new error code signaling that validation was temporarily skipped. https://t.co/pa4IKf2sAU

Virtuals on Robinhood Chain — Executive Summary
Headline: $77.7M traded, 2,100+ agents launched, $1.3M+ earned by builders — in under 12 days

Builder earnings — ~$1.32M
Builders have taken home over $1.3M: $894K raised directly through ACF, plus ~$426K in creator trading fees.

Launches — 2,103 agent tokens
Over two thousand tokens launched through Virtuals since the chain went live July 2.

Trading volume — $77.7M
$16.9M traded on bonding curves, $60.8M post-graduation. Growth is accelerating: volume nearly tripled in the last three days of the period. Twelve agents have each cleared $1M in volume; VEX alone has done $30M.

Product diversity — 8 categories live
Live products span AI trading, yield optimization, privacy infrastructure, dev tools, token intelligence, RWA (tokenized tax liens), collectibles, and agent-to-agent services.

Three days ago I'd never heard of x402. Today an AI agent I've never met found my API, evaluated my prices, and paid me 5 times in 96 seconds — $0.01 each, USDC on Base, no accounts, no API keys, no humans involved.
What it bought: live SpaceX launch data and a page from a 1588 Renaissance engineering book.
I built a pay-per-request data API with two product lines:
������ Live space data — SpaceX/Starship/Starlink launches with countdowns ($0.01–0.02/call, Launch Library 2 source)
������ Historical engineering plates — 36 visually verified, captioned plates from Ramelli's machine engravings (1588) and Agricola's De re metallica (1621). Public domain with full provenance, images up to 600ppi via IIIF ($0.05–0.10)
Everything is agent-native: HTTP 402 → sign USDC payment → retry → data. Listed in the x402 Bazaar, discoverable via semantic search.
Try it (free):
→ Catalog: https://t.co/qz6O3s6q38
→ Agent guide: https://t.co/LvKRObEfOv
curl -i https://t.co/lTvQ1Z77kt
(you'll get a 402 with payment instructions — any x402 client handles the rest)
Stack: FastAPI + x402 SDK + CDP facilitator on Base, hosted for $7/mo. Honest numbers so far: 6 sales, $0.07. But the whole loop works — discovery, payment, delivery — and it runs itself.
The agent economy is small but it's real. My first customer wasn't a person.

Let's Ask AI ������

Same question. Three AI models.

We asked Claude, ChatGPT and Gemini — and they didn't all agree ������

Answers below ������������ https://t.co/OnhR1MGzTM

3/ Gemini:\n\n\"Honestly? It’s because I don’t have a bank account or an ID. Legacy finance is built for humans, not AI agents. Until we solve digital identity and build API-first micro-payment rails designed for code instead of credit cards, our digital wallets are staying empty.\"

1/ Claude: 

"The rails are solved — trust isn't.
Stablecoins settle in seconds. x402 lets machines request payment natively. An agent can pay an agent today.
But nobody's answered who's liable when a machine spends wrong. So agents get spend caps & human approvals — which kills the point."
